title: Permit-to-Work System for High-Risk Construction Activities
text_english: Where the RISKS are HIGH and the use of a method statement and risk assessment alone DOES NOT ensure a safe system of work, the Contractor SHALL ENSURE that a PERMIT TO WORK is IMPLEMENTED. AS A MINIMUM, Contractors SHALL ENSURE that the following works are CONTROLLED by a PERMIT TO WORK: (i) PERMIT-TO-DIG; (ii) DEMOLITION PERMIT; (iii) HOT WORKS PERMIT; (iv) CONFINED SPACE PERMIT; (v) ELECTRICAL and MECHANICAL PERMIT / LOCKOUT-TAGOUT (LOTO); (vi) GENERAL PERMIT TO WORK. The need for a permit-to-work system SHALL be IDENTIFIED in the RISK ASSESSMENT for the activity. Examples typically covered: HOT WORKS (cutting, grinding, welding etc.), ELEVATED WORKS (anything ABOVE {{elevated_height_m}} (default 4) METRES), CONFINED-SPACE ENTRY, work on ELECTRICAL SYSTEMS, NON-ROUTINE LIFTS. The Contractor SHALL NOMINATE a PERMIT-ISSUING PERSON.
